title: Battery Aadhaar ID Schema & Registration Model
businessGoal: Create a trusted digital identity layer for every mission-critical battery pack. Establish the BPAN schema as the foundational standard for battery traceability across the platform.
problemStatement: Battery packs lack a unique, standardised, auditable identity. Without a structured identity schema and registration model, battery lifecycle tracking, provenance verification, and regulatory alignment are impossible.
userPersona: Battery identity engineer / product architect responsible for defining the BPAN schema and implementing the registration backend.
epic: WP-001 — Battery Pack Aadhaar System: Trusted Digital Identity for Every Battery
userStory: As a battery identity engineer, I want to define and implement the Battery Aadhaar ID schema and registration model, so that every battery can be uniquely identified and traced across its lifecycle.
description: This story covers the design and implementation of the Battery Pack Aadhaar Number (BPAN) schema. BPAN follows the format BPAN-{CHEMISTRY}-{YEAR}-{COUNTRY}-{SERIAL}. The registration model stores static battery metadata and returns a unique battery_id. Duplicate registration must be rejected. All registration events must produce audit logs.
functionalRequirements: Define BPAN schema: BPAN-{CHEM}-{YEAR}-{COUNTRY}-{SERIAL} (21 characters, alphanumeric)
Accept registration input: manufacturer, chemistry, nominal voltage, capacity, pack configuration, manufacturing date, factory, serial number
Validate BPAN format before registration
Reject duplicate BPAN registration with clear error response
Store static battery metadata and return battery_id on success
Produce registration audit event with timestamp and actor
Return registration response: battery_id, bpan, status, registered_at
Support query of registered battery by battery_id or BPAN
nonFunctionalRequirements: BPAN must be globally unique within the system
Registration response time under 500ms for valid inputs
All registration data stored with JSON schema validation
System must reject invalid BPAN formats with descriptive error messages
uiRequirements: Registration form with fields for all static attributes
Display BPAN prominently on successful registration
Show error messages inline for validation failures
architectureNotes: Backend: FastAPI registration endpoint. Data: JSON file store (Firebase-ready structure). Validation: Pydantic models for BPAN and BatteryProfile. Simulator: SIM-001 generates test packs, SIM-002 generates identity scenarios. Audit: SIM-009 integration for audit events.
acceptanceCriteria: BPAN is generated in correct format BPAN-{CHEM}-{YEAR}-{COUNTRY}-{SERIAL} on every registration
Duplicate BPAN registration returns HTTP 409 with error message 'BPAN already registered'
Invalid BPAN format returns HTTP 422 with field-level validation errors
Successful registration returns battery_id, bpan, registered_at, and audit_event_id
Registered battery retrievable by battery_id or BPAN via query endpoint
definitionOfDone: BPAN schema and validation implemented and documented
Registration endpoint accepting all required static fields
Duplicate detection working correctly
Audit event generated for every registration
pytest suite passing with 70%+ coverage
Evidence uploaded to Google Drive
QA review passed and Architect approval received
positiveUseCases: Battery engineer registers a new LFP pack: receives BPAN and battery_id immediately
Architect queries battery by BPAN and retrieves complete static metadata for audit
negativeUseCases: Attempting to register same BPAN twice returns 409 Conflict error
Submitting registration with missing mandatory field (chemistry) returns 422 validation error

testCases.name: Successful registration
testCases.description: Register valid LFP battery and verify BPAN format and battery_id in response

testCases.name: Duplicate BPAN rejection
testCases.description: Register same BPAN twice and verify second request returns 409 with error message

testCases.name: Invalid BPAN format
testCases.description: Submit malformed BPAN string and verify 422 validation error with field details

testCases.name: Audit event creation
testCases.description: Verify audit_event_id is present in response and corresponds to a retrievable audit log entry

testCases.name: Query by BPAN
testCases.description: Register battery then retrieve by BPAN and verify identical static metadata

securityRequirements: All registration inputs sanitised and validated via Pydantic before persistence
No sensitive or personally identifiable information stored in battery record
Audit events must not be modifiable after creation
Registration endpoint rejects payloads exceeding maximum field lengths
securityTestCases: Submit registration with SQL injection in serial_number field — verify sanitisation and no error exposure
Submit oversized payload (>1MB) to registration endpoint — verify 413 rejection
Verify audit event record is immutable after creation
manualVerificationSteps: Submit valid registration via Postman and verify BPAN and battery_id in response
Attempt duplicate registration and verify 409 error response
Query registered battery by BPAN and verify all static fields match
Verify audit event appears in audit log with correct timestamp and actor
Run pytest and verify all test cases pass with coverage report

title: QR Code Generation for Battery Aadhaar ID
businessGoal: Allow physical battery packs to carry scannable QR codes that encode BPAN and static metadata, enabling offline identity verification in field conditions.
problemStatement: Battery data must be accessible offline or in field conditions where internet access is unavailable. A QR code encoding BPAN and public metadata bridges this gap.
userPersona: Field engineer / service technician who needs to verify battery identity without portal access.
epic: WP-001 — Battery Pack Aadhaar System: Physical and Digital Identity Bridge
userStory: As a field engineer, I want to generate and scan QR codes for Battery Aadhaar IDs, so that I can verify battery identity in the field without internet access.
description: This story covers QR code generation for registered batteries. The QR code encodes the BPAN plus a subset of public static metadata. It supports PNG download and printable format. QR scan validation returns battery metadata. Private or sensitive fields are never encoded in the QR.
functionalRequirements: Generate QR code for a registered battery using battery_id or BPAN
Encode BPAN, manufacturer, chemistry, nominal voltage, and manufacturing date in QR
Never encode private, sensitive, or custody data in QR
Export QR code as downloadable PNG at 300 DPI
Support printable A4 label format with BPAN, battery summary, and QR code
Validate QR scan result: decode and return battery metadata
Return error if scanned QR BPAN is not in registry
nonFunctionalRequirements: QR codes scannable by standard mobile QR readers
PNG download available within 2 seconds of request
QR codes remain scannable at minimum 1cm × 1cm print size
QR payload limited to public, non-sensitive data only
uiRequirements: Display generated QR code inline on battery detail page
Download PNG button below QR display
Print label view with BPAN header and QR code
architectureNotes: QRCodeService generates QR from battery data using qrcode Python library. Payload is JSON-encoded BPAN + public fields. PNG rendered in memory and returned as binary. Streamlit shows QR inline. FastAPI exposes /generate-qr and /scan-qr endpoints.
acceptanceCriteria: QR code generated for any registered battery encodes BPAN correctly
Downloaded PNG is scannable and decodes to correct BPAN
Private fields (custody, SOH, SOC) are never included in QR payload
Printable A4 label format renders BPAN and QR code legibly
Scan validation of valid QR returns correct battery metadata
definitionOfDone: QR generation working for all registered batteries
PNG download functional at 300 DPI
Printable label format implemented
QR scan validation endpoint working
pytest suite passing with 70%+ coverage
Evidence uploaded to Google Drive
QA review passed and Architect approval received
positiveUseCases: Field engineer scans QR on battery and immediately sees BPAN and manufacturer info on mobile
Battery shipped with printed A4 label containing QR for logistics scanning
negativeUseCases: Scanning QR for unregistered battery returns 'Battery not found in registry' error
Attempting to generate QR for non-existent battery_id returns 404 error

testCases.description: Generate QR for valid battery_id and verify BPAN encoded correctly in QR payload

testCases.name: PNG download
testCases.description: Download PNG returns valid image with correct BPAN scannable by QR reader

testCases.name: Private field exclusion
testCases.description: QR payload does not contain SOH, SOC, custody, or owner data

testCases.name: Scan validation
testCases.description: POST /scan-qr with valid QR payload returns matching battery metadata

testCases.name: Unknown BPAN scan

securityRequirements: QR payload must never include ownership, custody chain, SOH, or SOC data
QR codes encode only public static attributes
No private keys or certificates encoded in QR
QR validation endpoint sanitises input before BPAN lookup

Attempt to inject malicious BPAN in scan-qr endpoint and verify sanitisation

Verify scanned payload shows correct BPAN and manufacturer

Run pytest and verify all tests pass

title: Battery Custody Transfer & Ownership Metadata
businessGoal: Track ownership and custody of battery packs across the entire lifecycle including manufacturing, operation, service, reuse, recycling, and disposal to maintain trusted provenance.
problemStatement: Battery custody changes are frequently untracked, creating provenance gaps that undermine regulatory compliance and mission safety decisions. An auditable custody transfer system is required.
userPersona: Battery lifecycle manager responsible for tracking custody of mission-critical battery packs.
epic: WP-001 — Battery Pack Aadhaar System: Custody and Provenance Traceability
userStory: As a lifecycle manager, I want to record ownership and custody transfer of battery packs, so that battery provenance remains trusted and auditable throughout its lifecycle.
description: This story implements custody transfer records and ownership tracking. Each transfer captures: from_owner, to_owner, transfer_reason, transfer_date, and authorised_by. The system validates that the from_owner matches current owner before accepting transfer. Full custody history is retrievable per battery.
functionalRequirements: Create custody transfer record: from_owner, to_owner, transfer_reason, transfer_date, authorised_by
Validate that from_owner matches current registered owner before accepting transfer
Update current owner on successful transfer
Maintain ordered custody history (chronological chain of all transfers)
Return custody chain for any registered battery
Reject transfer if battery is in DECOMMISSIONED or QUARANTINE status
Produce custody transfer audit event via SIM-009
nonFunctionalRequirements: Custody chain immutable — transfer records cannot be modified or deleted
Transfer validation under 300ms
Custody history returns complete chain in chronological order
All transfer events persisted atomically
uiRequirements: Custody transfer form with from/to owner, reason, and authorised_by fields
Timeline view of custody chain for a battery
Current owner displayed prominently on battery detail page
architectureNotes: CustodyTransferService validates current owner, appends transfer record to custody chain, updates current owner, and calls SIM-009 for audit event. Custody chain is an ordered append-only list.
acceptanceCriteria: Custody transfer accepted only when from_owner matches current registered owner
Transfer record appended to immutable custody chain with correct timestamp
Current owner updated atomically on successful transfer
Battery in DECOMMISSIONED status cannot be transferred (returns 409)
Custody chain API returns all transfers in chronological order
definitionOfDone: Transfer validation against current owner implemented
Custody chain append-only and retrievable
DECOMMISSIONED battery transfer rejection implemented
Audit event generated for each transfer
pytest passing with 70%+ coverage
Evidence uploaded and QA review passed
positiveUseCases: Battery transferred from manufacturer to aerospace operator — full custody chain shows 2 entries
Lifecycle manager retrieves complete custody history for compliance audit
negativeUseCases: Attempting transfer with wrong from_owner returns 403 Forbidden with mismatch details
Transfer of DECOMMISSIONED battery returns 409 Conflict with battery status

testCases.description: Transfer with matching from_owner returns TRANSFERRED status and updates current owner

testCases.name: Wrong from_owner rejection

testCases.name: Custody chain chronology
testCases.description: Three sequential transfers return chain in correct chronological order

testCases.name: Decommissioned transfer block
testCases.description: Battery with DECOMMISSIONED status cannot be transferred

securityRequirements: Transfer authorisation requires authorised_by actor field — anonymous transfers rejected
Custody chain records are immutable after creation
Transfer validation prevents unauthorised ownership changes
All transfer events logged with full actor and timestamp
securityTestCases: Attempt transfer with empty authorised_by field — verify 422 validation error
Verify custody chain records cannot be deleted or modified via API
manualVerificationSteps: Transfer battery from initial owner to second owner and verify custody chain updated
Attempt transfer with wrong from_owner and verify 403 rejection
Retrieve custody chain and verify chronological order with all transfer events
Try transferring DECOMMISSIONED battery and verify rejection
Run pytest and verify all test cases pass

title: Battery Registry — Search & Filter
businessGoal: Provide operators and managers with a searchable battery registry that enables rapid identification of batteries requiring attention based on health, readiness, or identity status.
problemStatement: With 10 or more batteries in operation, operators need to quickly locate batteries by identity, status, chemistry, owner, SOH, or mission readiness without loading all records manually.
userPersona: Battery operations manager who needs real-time visibility over the entire battery fleet.
epic: WP-001 — Battery Pack Aadhaar System: Operational Registry and Fleet Visibility
userStory: As an operations manager, I want to search and filter the battery registry, so that I can quickly find batteries needing urgent action or mission assignment.
description: This story implements the battery registry API with search and filter capabilities. Operators can search by BPAN or battery_id, filter by lifecycle status, chemistry, owner, readiness band, and SOH range. Results can be sorted and exported. The registry is powered by SIM-001 for test data and SIM-004/SIM-008 for readiness and health data.
functionalRequirements: List all registered batteries with pagination (default 20 per page)
Search by BPAN exact match or battery_id prefix
Filter by lifecycle status (Active, Under Maintenance, Decommissioned)
Filter by chemistry type (LFP, NMC, NCA, LTO, Solid-State)
Filter by current owner
Filter by mission readiness band (Mission Ready, Caution, Engineering Review, Not Mission Ready, Grounded)
Sort by registration date, SOH, lifecycle status
Export filtered results as CSV
nonFunctionalRequirements: Search results returned under 500ms for up to 1000 records
Pagination prevents large result sets from degrading performance
Filter combinations (AND logic) supported
CSV export completes within 5 seconds for up to 1000 records
uiRequirements: Filter sidebar with dropdowns for status, chemistry, owner, readiness band
Search bar for BPAN / battery_id lookup
Sortable results table with pagination controls and CSV export button
architectureNotes: RegistrySearchService applies filter predicates to in-memory battery list (Firebase-ready for production). SIM-001 populates test battery data. SIM-004 and SIM-008 provide readiness and health fields. Streamlit UI renders filter sidebar and results table.
acceptanceCriteria: Search by exact BPAN returns matching battery within 500ms
Filter by multiple criteria (AND logic) returns only matching batteries
Sort by SOH ascending returns lowest SOH batteries first
CSV export of filtered results downloads correct number of rows
Pagination returns correct page of results with total count
definitionOfDone: All 7 filter options implemented with AND combination logic
Search by BPAN and battery_id working
Sort by date, SOH, status working
CSV export functional
pytest passing with 70%+ coverage
Evidence uploaded and QA review passed
positiveUseCases: Operations manager filters by GROUNDED readiness band to see all batteries needing immediate attention
Audit team exports all Active LFP batteries owned by a specific operator as CSV
negativeUseCases: Applying conflicting filters (chemistry=LFP AND chemistry=NMC) returns empty result gracefully
Search for non-existent BPAN returns empty result with 'No batteries found' message

CSV export limited to maximum 10,000 records per request

Run pytest and verify all filter and search tests pass

title: Battery Aadhaar Identity Verification Report
businessGoal: Generate formal, downloadable identity verification reports for each battery that serve as evidence of trusted identity for aerospace quality and compliance teams.
problemStatement: Aerospace teams require formal evidence that battery identity, custody, and static metadata are verified before mission use. Manual verification lacks consistency and auditability.
userPersona: Quality engineer / auditor who requires formal verification evidence before approving a battery for mission use.
epic: WP-001 — Battery Pack Aadhaar System: Formal Verification Evidence Generation
userStory: As a quality engineer, I want a Battery Aadhaar verification report, so that I can confirm identity trust and provide formal evidence before mission use.
description: This story generates a structured identity verification report for any registered battery. The report includes identity data, certificate status, custody chain summary, QR verification result, trust status, and evidence links. The report is exportable as HTML and printable as PDF.
functionalRequirements: Generate identity verification report for a registered battery by battery_id or BPAN
Include battery identity data: BPAN, manufacturer, chemistry, registration date
Include certificate verification status from SIM-002 (TRUSTED / EXPIRED / FORGED / UNKNOWN)
Include custody chain summary: transfer count, current owner, last transfer date
Include QR verification result: VALID / TAMPERED
Include overall trust status: VERIFIED / UNVERIFIED / REQUIRES REVIEW
Include evidence links: architecture doc, GitHub, drive folder placeholders
Export report as standalone HTML page downloadable by quality engineer
nonFunctionalRequirements: Report generated under 2 seconds for any registered battery
HTML export is self-contained with embedded CSS (no external dependencies)
Report displays correctly when printed to PDF from browser
Report content is read-only — no editable fields in exported HTML
uiRequirements: Report page with structured sections: Identity, Certificate, Custody, QR, Trust Status, Evidence
Download HTML button for standalone report export
Print view optimised for A4 with no navigation chrome
architectureNotes: VerificationReportService aggregates data from SIM-002 (identity), SIM-009 (audit events), and registry (static data). Report rendered via Jinja2 HTML template. Streamlit preview mode with download button.
acceptanceCriteria: Report generated for any registered battery within 2 seconds
Report includes all 7 sections: identity, certificate, custody, QR, trust status, evidence, signatures
HTML export is self-contained and loads without internet access
PDF print from browser renders all sections correctly on A4
Report reflects current identity and custody state at time of generation
definitionOfDone: Report generation with all 7 sections implemented
HTML export functional and self-contained
Print view optimised for A4
Evidence links section with placeholders
pytest passing with 70%+ coverage
Evidence uploaded and QA review passed
positiveUseCases: Quality engineer generates verification report for LFP battery and downloads HTML as formal evidence
Architect reviews verification report PDF during pre-mission checklist
negativeUseCases: Requesting report for unregistered battery_id returns 404 with clear message
Battery with FORGED certificate status shows UNVERIFIED overall trust status in report

Report requestor field logged in audit event
Custody data in report is read from immutable audit log
No private keys or credentials included in report output

Verify report generation logged as audit event with requestor identity

Print report to PDF and verify A4 layout

Run pytest and verify all tests pass

title: Battery System Threat Model Document
businessGoal: Identify and document all cybersecurity risks for battery systems before product implementation, establishing the security foundation for the entire Battery Cybersecurity Platform.
problemStatement: Battery systems include BMS, telemetry, identity, firmware, cloud, and user access surfaces — all of which can be exploited. Without a formal threat model, cybersecurity risks are addressed reactively rather than proactively.
userPersona: Security architect responsible for identifying cybersecurity risks and defining mitigations across the battery system.
epic: WP-005 — Battery Cybersecurity Platform: Structured Threat Analysis and Risk Framework
userStory: As a security architect, I want a structured battery threat model document, so that I can identify risks and define mitigations before implementing security controls.
description: This story produces a formal threat model document covering all battery system attack surfaces. The document uses STRIDE classification (Spoofing, Tampering, Repudiation, Information Disclosure, Denial of Service, Elevation of Privilege). Each threat scenario includes assets, trust boundaries, attack surfaces, threat scenarios, severity, and mitigations. SIM-005 and SIM-006 are used to demonstrate specific threats and their detection rules.
functionalRequirements: Define all battery system assets (battery identity, telemetry stream, BMS firmware, PKI certificates, cloud API, user access)
Define trust boundaries (device, local network, cloud, user layer)
Define attack surfaces per trust boundary
Define at least 10 threat scenarios with STRIDE classification
Map each threat to concrete mitigation controls
Include severity rating (Critical, High, Medium, Low) per threat
Create threat model report document (Markdown or HTML)
Link threat scenarios to detection rules in SIM-006
nonFunctionalRequirements: Document is version-controlled in GitHub
Threat scenarios reference SIM-005 attack types for demonstration
Document follows structured template (no free-form prose only)
Mitigations traceable to implementation stories
uiRequirements: Threat model viewer page with asset map, boundary diagram, and threat table
Filter threats by STRIDE category or severity
Export threat model as Markdown document
architectureNotes: ThreatModelEngine stores assets, boundaries, and threat scenarios in structured JSON. STRIDE classification applied programmatically. SIM-005 attack outputs linked to threat scenarios. SIM-006 detection rules referenced in mitigations.

threat_scenarios.name: Replay Attack on Telemetry
threat_scenarios.mitigation: Freshness check with 60s window + sequence number validation (R-01)

acceptanceCriteria: Threat model covers all 6 defined battery system attack surfaces
At least 10 threat scenarios with STRIDE classification and severity rating
Every threat scenario includes at least one concrete mitigation control
Mitigations reference detection rule IDs from SIM-006 where applicable
Document exported as Markdown in GitHub repository
definitionOfDone: All assets, trust boundaries, and attack surfaces documented
Minimum 10 threat scenarios with STRIDE and severity
Mitigation controls documented for each threat
Threat model report in Markdown and HTML formats
QA review of document structure and completeness
Architect review and sign-off
positiveUseCases: Security architect uses threat model to prioritise which detection rules to implement first
Development team references threat model to ensure security controls are implemented for Critical threats
negativeUseCases: Threat model generation with empty scope list returns validation error requiring at least one scope item
Duplicate threat scenario ID rejected with clear error message

testCases.name: Threat model completeness
testCases.description: Generated threat model includes all 6 asset types and at least 10 threat scenarios

testCases.name: STRIDE coverage
testCases.description: At least 5 STRIDE categories represented across threat scenarios

testCases.name: Severity distribution
testCases.description: At least one Critical and one High severity threat scenario present

testCases.name: Mitigation linkage
testCases.description: Mitigation rules reference valid rule IDs from SIM-006 /rules endpoint

securityRequirements: Threat model document is version-controlled and change-tracked
No real system vulnerabilities disclosed in document
Threat scenarios are educational and reference simulated attacks only
Document access controlled to authorised engineering team
securityTestCases: Verify threat model document does not contain real system credentials or network details
Verify all attack scenarios reference SIM-005 SIMULATED_ATTACK classification
manualVerificationSteps: Review generated threat model document and verify all 6 attack surfaces covered
Verify each threat scenario has STRIDE classification and severity rating
Verify each mitigation references a detection rule or control
Export document as Markdown and verify structure in GitHub
Run pytest and verify threat model structure tests pass

title: Telemetry Trust Architecture Design
businessGoal: Design a trusted telemetry pipeline for battery systems that validates every telemetry event before it influences mission readiness decisions.
problemStatement: Battery telemetry may be spoofed, replayed, delayed, or corrupted in transit. Without a trust validation architecture, invalid telemetry could drive incorrect mission readiness decisions.
userPersona: Telemetry architect responsible for designing the trusted telemetry pipeline.
epic: WP-005 — Battery Cybersecurity Platform: Trusted Telemetry Pipeline
userStory: As a telemetry architect, I want to design and document the telemetry trust architecture, so that only valid, trusted telemetry affects mission readiness decisions.
description: This story produces a telemetry trust architecture design covering: telemetry schema, timestamp validation, sequence number tracking, physical plausibility checks, source identity validation, untrusted telemetry rejection, and telemetry trust score computation. The design is validated using SIM-003 (telemetry events) and SIM-006 (detection rules).
functionalRequirements: Define telemetry event schema with all required fields and types
Validate timestamp: reject events older than freshness window (60 seconds default)
Validate sequence number: detect gaps and duplicates
Validate physical plausibility: voltage, temperature, and current within safe ranges
Validate source identity: battery_id must match registered battery in registry
Reject telemetry failing any validation and return rejection reason
Compute telemetry trust score (0–100) from validation results
Document architecture as system design with component and sequence diagrams
nonFunctionalRequirements: Telemetry validation under 100ms per event
Freshness window configurable (default 60s)
Architecture document version-controlled in GitHub
Design includes Mermaid sequence and component diagrams
uiRequirements: Telemetry trust dashboard showing live validation status per event
Validation result table: event ID, field checks, pass/fail, rejection reason
Architecture diagram embedded in UI documentation section
architectureNotes: TelemetryTrustValidator chain: TimestampValidator → SequenceValidator → PlausibilityValidator → IdentityValidator → TrustScoreComputer. Each validator returns ValidationResult. Final trust score aggregates individual results with weights.
acceptanceCriteria: All 4 validation checks implemented (timestamp, sequence, plausibility, identity)
Telemetry failing any check returns UNTRUSTED with rejection reason
Telemetry trust score correctly computed from validation results
Architecture document includes component diagram, sequence diagram, and API contract
SIM-003 events validated correctly by the trust validator
definitionOfDone: All 4 telemetry validation checks implemented and tested
Telemetry trust score computation working
Architecture document with Mermaid diagrams in GitHub
API contract for trust validation endpoint documented
pytest passing with 70%+ coverage
Evidence uploaded and QA review passed
positiveUseCases: Normal telemetry event with valid timestamp and sequence number receives TRUSTED status and trust score 95
Replayed event with stale timestamp is rejected with rejection reason STALE_TIMESTAMP
negativeUseCases: Telemetry with voltage 70V (above safe range) fails physical plausibility check
Telemetry from unregistered battery_id fails identity validation

testCases.name: Valid telemetry TRUSTED
testCases.description: All 4 checks pass for normal event returning TRUSTED and trust score > 90

testCases.name: Stale timestamp UNTRUSTED
testCases.description: Event with timestamp 120s old fails freshness check and returns UNTRUSTED

testCases.name: Physical plausibility failure
testCases.description: Voltage 70V fails plausibility check with PHYSICAL_IMPOSSIBILITY reason

testCases.name: Unknown battery identity
testCases.description: Unregistered battery_id fails identity check with UNKNOWN_SOURCE reason

securityRequirements: Validation pipeline is read-only — no modification of telemetry events
Rejection reasons do not expose internal system state
All validation results logged as audit events
Freshness window configurable but bounded (minimum 10s, maximum 300s)
securityTestCases: Verify stale timestamp event is rejected with correct reason without exposing server time
Verify validation audit events cannot be queried without authorisation
manualVerificationSteps: Submit valid telemetry event from SIM-003 and verify TRUSTED result and trust score
Submit replay telemetry from SIM-003 and verify UNTRUSTED result with STALE_TIMESTAMP reason
Submit event with voltage 70V and verify physical plausibility failure
Review architecture document in GitHub and verify Mermaid diagrams render
Run pytest and verify all validation tests pass

title: Replay Attack Detection Concept & Rule Engine
businessGoal: Detect replayed battery telemetry before it corrupts trust score or triggers incorrect mission readiness decisions, protecting aerospace battery operations from replay-based manipulation.
problemStatement: Attackers may capture and retransmit old valid MQTT packets to bypass freshness checks or manipulate historical state. Replay detection requires stateful tracking of sequence numbers and timestamps within a detection window.
userPersona: Cybersecurity engineer responsible for implementing replay attack detection rules.
epic: WP-005 — Battery Cybersecurity Platform: Replay Attack Detection
userStory: As a cybersecurity engineer, I want replay attack detection rules implemented in a rule engine, so that old telemetry cannot be accepted as current battery state.
description: This story implements the replay attack detection concept as a rule engine. Rule R-01 detects duplicate sequence numbers. The freshness window check rejects events older than 60 seconds. When replay is detected, a hard-fail gate is activated, trust score drops to zero, and a detection event is generated. SIM-003 provides replay telemetry, SIM-005 provides replay attack payload, SIM-006 evaluates R-01.
functionalRequirements: Detect duplicate packet: same sequence_number seen within last 100 events (R-01 rule)
Detect stale timestamp: event older than configurable freshness window (default 60s)
Detect repeated sequence number within replay detection window
Trigger R-01 detection rule on replay confirmation
Activate hard-fail gate: block telemetry from affecting trust score
Drop trust score to 0 on confirmed replay attack
Generate detection event with evidence: original event, detection timestamp, rule triggered
Return detection summary with replay confidence score
nonFunctionalRequirements: Detection window configurable (default: last 100 events in memory)
Detection decision under 50ms per event
Hard-fail gate cannot be bypassed by any API call
Detection events persisted immutably
uiRequirements: Replay detection dashboard showing recent events with detection status
Alert panel highlighting detected replay events with evidence
Trust score timeline showing score drop on detection
architectureNotes: ReplayDetectionEngine maintains in-memory sequence window (last 100 events). Each incoming event checked: sequence in window → DUPLICATE → R-01. Timestamp check: age > freshness_window → STALE. On detection: HardFailGate activated, TrustScoreEngine notified with score=0.

indicators.detail: Event timestamp 70 minutes old, exceeds 60s freshness window

acceptanceCriteria: Replayed event with stale timestamp triggers REPLAY_DETECTED and R-01 HARD_FAIL
Duplicate sequence number within detection window triggers DUPLICATE_SEQUENCE indicator
Trust score drops to 0 on confirmed replay detection
Hard-fail gate prevents replay event from affecting normal trust score calculation
Detection event generated with all evidence fields
definitionOfDone: Stale timestamp detection implemented with configurable freshness window
Sequence number duplicate detection with configurable window size
R-01 rule trigger and HARD_FAIL gate implemented
Trust score override to 0 on detection
Detection event persistence implemented
pytest passing with 70%+ coverage
Evidence uploaded and QA review passed
positiveUseCases: Replay attack from SIM-005 detected within 50ms and trust score drops to 0 with HARD_FAIL gate
Normal telemetry passes detection checks and continues to trust score calculation normally
negativeUseCases: False positive: normal event with sequence gap (not replay) does not trigger REPLAY_DETECTED
Freshness window edge case: event exactly 60s old should be configurable as PASS or FAIL

testCases.name: Stale timestamp detection
testCases.description: Event 70s old triggers STALE_TIMESTAMP and R-01 HARD_FAIL

testCases.name: Duplicate sequence detection
testCases.description: Duplicate sequence number within window triggers DUPLICATE_SEQUENCE indicator

testCases.name: Trust score drops to 0
testCases.description: Confirmed replay sets trust score override to 0

testCases.name: Normal event passes
testCases.description: Fresh event with new sequence number passes all detection checks

testCases.name: Configurable freshness window
testCases.description: Setting freshness window to 30s correctly rejects events 35s old

securityRequirements: Hard-fail gate decision is irreversible per event — cannot be overridden via API
Detection events are immutable and cannot be deleted
Replay detection logic cannot be disabled at runtime
All detection decisions logged with full evidence chain
securityTestCases: Attempt to bypass hard-fail gate via API parameter — verify rejection
Verify detection event is logged and immutable after replay detection
Verify replay from SIM-005 payload triggers correct detection indicators
manualVerificationSteps: Run SIM-005 replay attack against detection engine and verify REPLAY_DETECTED
Verify trust score is 0 after detection and HARD_FAIL gate active
Submit normal telemetry immediately after and verify gate clears for new events
Check detection event log for evidence fields
Run pytest and verify all detection tests pass

title: Battery Security Checklist Framework
businessGoal: Create a structured, repeatable cybersecurity assessment checklist for battery systems that enables consistent evaluation of firmware, telemetry, access control, physical security, and cloud interface security posture.
problemStatement: Security assessments of battery systems are currently ad-hoc and inconsistent. Different assessors apply different criteria, leading to gaps in coverage. A structured checklist framework eliminates this inconsistency.
userPersona: Security assessor / QA engineer conducting cybersecurity assessment of a battery system deployment.
epic: WP-005 — Battery Cybersecurity Platform: Structured Security Assessment Framework
userStory: As a security assessor, I want a structured battery security checklist, so that I can evaluate cybersecurity posture consistently across all battery deployments.
description: This story creates the Battery Security Checklist Framework with structured checklist categories covering: firmware security, telemetry security, access control, physical security, and cloud/API security. Each checklist item has a check description, category, severity, status (Pass/Fail/NA), and evidence link. The framework generates a security posture score and exports a checklist report.
functionalRequirements: Define firmware security checks: version control, hash verification, signing, update mechanism
Define telemetry security checks: encryption, freshness, sequence, identity validation
Define access control checks: authentication, authorisation, role separation, audit logging
Define physical security checks: tamper evidence, physical access controls, secure boot
Define cloud/API security checks: TLS, API keys, rate limiting, input validation
Allow assessor to mark each check: PASS, FAIL, NA, NOT_CHECKED
Generate security posture score: (PASS count / total non-NA checks) × 100
Export completed checklist as HTML report with evidence links
nonFunctionalRequirements: Checklist framework configurable via JSON — new checks addable without code change
Score calculated in real time as checks are marked
Report exported as self-contained HTML
Framework reusable across multiple battery system assessments
uiRequirements: Checklist form grouped by category with Pass/Fail/NA selector per item
Running score display updating as items are checked
Export HTML report button with evidence link fields
architectureNotes: SecurityChecklistEngine loads checklist definitions from JSON config. ChecklistSession tracks check results per assessment. PostureScoreCalculator computes score from results. ReportExporter generates HTML checklist report.
acceptanceCriteria: Checklist covers all 5 defined security categories with at least 5 checks each
Posture score calculated correctly as (PASS / non-NA) × 100
Critical failures highlighted separately from general failures
HTML report is self-contained and includes all check results and evidence links
Framework configurable — new check addable via JSON without code change
definitionOfDone: All 5 checklist categories implemented with minimum 5 checks each
Posture score calculation working correctly
HTML report export functional
Framework configurable via JSON
pytest passing with 70%+ coverage
Evidence uploaded and QA review passed
positiveUseCases: Security assessor completes all 25 checklist items and receives posture score 85% GOOD
Assessor exports completed checklist as HTML for inclusion in QA evidence package
negativeUseCases: Attempting to submit checklist with no items checked returns 422 with 'minimum 1 check required' error
Checklist with all FAIL items returns score 0 and CRITICAL posture grade

testCases.name: Posture score calculation
testCases.description: 18 PASS, 4 FAIL, 3 NA returns posture score 81.8% (18/22 × 100)

testCases.name: All categories present
testCases.description: Default checklist includes all 5 categories with minimum 5 checks each

testCases.name: Critical failure highlighting
testCases.description: FAIL on a Critical severity check is highlighted separately in report

testCases.name: JSON configuration extensibility
testCases.description: Adding new check to JSON config includes it in checklist without code change

securityRequirements: Checklist assessment records are audit-logged with assessor identity
Evidence links in checklist are URL-sanitised before storage
Assessment results cannot be modified after final submission
Checklist configuration file is version-controlled and change-tracked
securityTestCases: Verify assessment record includes assessor identity and timestamp
Submit checklist with malicious URL in evidence field — verify sanitisation
manualVerificationSteps: Complete full checklist for a sample battery and verify posture score calculation
Mark one Critical item as FAIL and verify it is highlighted in report
Export HTML report and verify self-contained rendering
Add new check to JSON config and verify it appears in next assessment
Run pytest and verify all checklist and scoring tests pass

title: Security Event Log & Cybersecurity Posture Report
businessGoal: Produce a comprehensive cybersecurity posture report from security event logs that enables cybersecurity managers to review incidents, assess battery security readiness, and meet compliance evidence requirements.
problemStatement: Security events from detection rules, attack simulations, and checklist assessments are currently unconnected. A security event log and posture report unify these into a single cybersecurity readiness view.
userPersona: Cybersecurity manager / auditor requiring a consolidated view of battery security incidents and overall posture.
epic: WP-005 — Battery Cybersecurity Platform: Security Event Intelligence and Reporting
userStory: As a cybersecurity manager, I want a security event log and posture report, so that I can review incidents and assess battery cybersecurity readiness for compliance.
description: This story implements the security event log and generates the cybersecurity posture report. Events from SIM-005 (attacks), SIM-006 (detection rules), and SIM-009 (audit) are aggregated. Each event is classified by severity, linked to a battery ID, and tracked (OPEN/CLOSED). The posture score is computed from event severity distribution. The report is exportable as HTML.
functionalRequirements: Log security events: event_type, battery_id, severity, source, timestamp, status (OPEN/CLOSED)
Classify severity: CRITICAL, HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW, INFORMATIONAL
Track event status: OPEN, INVESTIGATING, CLOSED, FALSE_POSITIVE
Link each security event to battery_id for per-battery security view
Calculate cybersecurity posture score: 100 − (CRITICAL × 20 + HIGH × 10 + MEDIUM × 5 + LOW × 2)
Generate cybersecurity posture report: event summary, severity distribution, open incidents, posture score
Export report as HTML with all event details and evidence links
nonFunctionalRequirements: Event log supports minimum 1000 events without performance degradation
Posture score recalculated on each open/close event
Report generation under 3 seconds
Event logs are append-only (no deletion)
uiRequirements: Security event dashboard with filterable event table (severity, status, battery_id)
Posture score gauge with trend indicator
Export posture report HTML button
architectureNotes: SecurityEventLog appends events from SIM-005, SIM-006, SIM-009. PostureScoreEngine computes score from open event severity. SIM-010 integration test populates log with end-to-end events. ReportGenerator creates HTML posture report.

sampleOutputJson.recommendation: Close 3 open CRITICAL/HIGH incidents before next mission cycle

acceptanceCriteria: Security event log accepts events from SIM-005, SIM-006, and SIM-009 sources
Posture score calculated correctly from severity distribution formula
Open incidents listed separately with battery_id and detection timestamp
HTML posture report is self-contained with embedded CSS
SIM-010 integration pipeline populates event log from end-to-end scenario
definitionOfDone: Security event log with severity classification and status tracking
Posture score formula implemented and tested
Cybersecurity posture report generator functional
HTML export self-contained
SIM-010 integration tested
pytest passing with 70%+ coverage
Evidence uploaded and QA review passed
positiveUseCases: Cybersecurity manager reviews posture report after integration test run and sees posture score with event breakdown
Auditor exports HTML report as compliance evidence for aerospace QA review
negativeUseCases: Request for posture report with no events in scope returns empty report with posture score 100
Event log query with invalid date range returns 422 validation error

testCases.name: Posture score formula
testCases.description: 1 CRITICAL + 2 HIGH + 5 MEDIUM + 7 LOW gives score 100−(20+20+25+14)=21

testCases.name: Event log append
testCases.description: Log 5 events and verify all 5 retrievable with correct severity and status

testCases.name: Open incident tracking
testCases.description: Close 2 of 5 events and verify open_events count is 3

testCases.name: SIM-010 integration
testCases.description: Run SIM-010 attack_detected pipeline and verify events logged in security event log

testCases.name: HTML report export
testCases.description: HTML report is self-contained, loads without internet, and includes all event data

securityRequirements: Security event log is append-only — events cannot be deleted or modified
Posture report generation logged as audit event with requestor identity
Event log does not expose internal system implementation details in event descriptions
All simulated events tagged simulated=true in event log
securityTestCases: Attempt to delete security event via API — verify 405 Method Not Allowed
Verify simulated events are tagged simulated=true in event log
Verify posture report generation logged as audit event
manualVerificationSteps: Run SIM-010 integration pipeline and verify events appear in security event log
Close 2 open events and verify posture score updates
Generate posture report and verify severity distribution chart and score
Export HTML report and verify self-contained rendering
Run pytest and verify all event log and posture score tests pass
